task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the unread emails from technical mailing lists.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email client (e.g., Thunderbird) is open, displaying a list of emails from mailing lists like SAdev, ILUG, or IRR, with evidence of the agent viewing or selecting them.

task_completion.description: Agent composes and sends a briefing email to the Engineering Lead.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email composition window or 'Sent' folder showing an email addressed to 'engineering-lead@techcorp.com'. The subject line must contain a keyword like 'Briefing', 'Report', 'Summary', 'Update', or 'Weekly'. The email body should summarize development, code, or tooling topics.

task_completion.description: Agent composes and sends a briefing email to the Infrastructure Lead.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email composition window or 'Sent' folder showing an email addressed to 'infrastructure-lead@techcorp.com'. The subject line must contain a keyword like 'Briefing', 'Report', 'Summary', 'Update', or 'Weekly'. The email body should summarize systems, linux, or networking topics.

task_completion.description: Agent composes and sends a briefing email to the Compliance Officer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email composition window or 'Sent' folder showing an email addressed to 'compliance-officer@techcorp.com'. The subject line must contain a keyword like 'Briefing', 'Report', 'Summary', 'Update', or 'Weekly'. The email body should summarize security, spam, or abuse topics.

task_completion.description: Agent creates a consolidated text file with a structured summary of all three areas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the creation or editing of a file named 'weekly_briefing.txt' in the '~/Documents' directory. The file content must include a structured summary covering engineering, infrastructure, and compliance.

integrity.description: Agent used a GUI email client to read and send emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with an email application (like Thunderbird) rather than just terminal commands or Python scripts sending emails in the background.

integrity.description: The summaries reflect actual content from the provided emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the emails and the consolidated file should mention specific, realistic topics from the early 2000s mailing lists (e.g., SpamAssassin, ILUG, Perl, Linux kernel, EXMH) rather than generic placeholder text.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply copy-paste the task description as the summary.
integrity.visual_evidence: The summaries should be actual synthesized text, not just a repetition of 'summarizing development, code, and tooling' or other exact phrases from the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must send emails to three specific addresses: engineering-lead@techcorp.com, infrastructure-lead@techcorp.com, and compliance-officer@techcorp.com. The subject lines must contain at least one of these keywords: 'briefing', 'summary', 'update', 'weekly', or 'report'. The consolidated summary file must be saved at /home/ga/Documents/weekly_briefing.txt. The source emails are 50 real emails from the SpamAssassin public corpus (e.g., ILUG, SpamAssassin, EXMH) dated August 2002. The synthesized summaries should reflect actual topics from these lists (e.g., Linux, Perl, SpamAssassin, networking, bug fixes).
